subject: Databases
title: Time-Series Databases
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Time-Series Databases.

hook.text: A server emits a metric every second. That's 86,400 data points per day, per server. Time-series databases handle it.

buildup.text: Time-series databases optimize for append-heavy, time-ordered data. Writes are fast; old data compresses.

discovery.text: InfluxDB, TimescaleDB, and Prometheus power monitoring dashboards across the industry.

twist.text: They shine at range queries: give me CPU usage for the last hour. Traditional DBs struggle at this scale.

climax.text: IoT, finance, and observability all depend on time-series data. The volume is only growing.

punchline.text: When data flows with time, use a time-series database.

quiz.question: What makes time-series databases different from general-purpose ones?
options: They optimize for append-heavy, time-ordered data with fast range queries
They don't support any queries at all
